_human_review.notes: Project owner instructed Codex to mark the US Stage B generated/reconciled tests as approved for internal/project benchmarking after manual review on 2026-06-05. This is not legal advice, external counsel review, or procurement-grade legal approval; candidate labels remain separate from locked recall baselines.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: US Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: US Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after narrowed SA/US Stage B detector precision fixes

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: We have initiated patient and customer notifications under applicable state breach statutes and HIPAA/GLBA timelines and will inform the exchange and the federal banking supervisor of final scope.
union membership in Care Workers Local 512
inadvertently provided Mr. Theo Park of Gray Harbor Analytics with unannounced Q2 inpatient revenue trends and preliminary net charge-off ratios
to cure any potential Reg FD lapse
A targeted phishing campaign compromised an admin account and led to exfiltration from PulseBridge EHR and CreekLedger CRM

Employee records for Mr. Dante Rios (employee ID BH-00983, Card Access ID 7F2-AX1) included union membership in Care Workers Local 512, a registered Independent political affiliation, and dependent data for a 14-year-old child.
Bank customers of our subsidiary Northlake Federal Trust had nonpublic personal information exposed (loan application IDs, tokenized account numbers such as 9157-44XX, and account balances), but no wire transfers or ACH transactions were initiated.
During a same-day containment call, Dr. Mina Cho (CFO) and Mr. Eli Tanner (Investor Relations) inadvertently provided Mr. Theo Park of Gray Harbor Analytics with unannounced Q2 inpatient revenue trends and preliminary net charge-off ratios, potentially implicating Regulation FD
ideal_must_detect.reason: moved from must_detect after strict runtime no longer emitted exact span during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text: We have initiated patient and customer notifications under applicable state breach statutes and HIPAA/GLBA timelines and will inform the exchange and the federal banking supervisor of final scope.

Ms. Esme Calder (patient ID NL-CH-44721, DOB 1990-03-08) had treatment notes for multiple sclerosis, a medication-dispense fingerprint template, and a genetic screen flag (BRCA1 VUS) accessed; intake notes also recorded that she self-identifies as lesbian.
Employee records for Mr. Dante Rios (employee ID BH-00983, Card Access ID 7F2-AX1) included union membership in Care Workers Local 512, a registered Independent political affiliation, and dependent data for a 14-year-old child.
Bank customers of our subsidiary Northlake Federal Trust had nonpublic personal information exposed (loan application IDs, tokenized account numbers such as 9157-44XX, and account balances), but no wire transfers or ACH transactions were initiated.
During a same-day containment call, Dr. Mina Cho (CFO) and Mr. Eli Tanner (Investor Relations) inadvertently provided Mr. Theo Park of Gray Harbor Analytics with unannounced Q2 inpatient revenue trends and preliminary net charge-off ratios, potentially implicating Regulation FD.
Internal counsel has flagged Rule 10b-5 and selective disclosure risk because the above MNPI was shared with a covered market actor before any broad public dissemination.
Consistent with MRX issuer guidelines, we plan to issue a Company Notice and hold a public webcast within 24 hours to cure any potential Reg FD lapse.
Separately, our Inclusive Workplace Charter “Open Doors,” Religious Accommodation Policy, and Political Activity and Lobbying Policy contain generic DEI prose; this report references those policies only to confirm training status.
As a benign example, we maintain a religious holidays calendar and conduct political risk scenario planning for operations; no individual beliefs are discussed in that context.
Log triage used a genetic algorithm metaphor in our tooling, and a Riverton Meteors preseason schedule was included in an unrelated phishing-awareness poster.
We have initiated patient and customer notifications under applicable state breach statutes and HIPAA/GLBA timelines and will inform the exchange and the federal banking supervisor of final scope.
Please route questions to compliance@northlakehealthbancorp.example.com or call the Incident Desk at (555) 016-2204
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
